urllib.robotparser/RobotFileParser.name: RobotFileParser
urllib.robotparser/RobotFileParser.description: This class provides methods to read, parse and answer questions about the robots.txt file at url. set_url(url)¶ Sets the URL referring to a robots.txt file. read()¶ Reads the robots.txt URL and feeds it to the parser. parse(lines)¶ Parses the lines argument. can_fetch(useragent, url)¶ Returns True if the useragent is allowed to fetch the url according to the rules contained in the parsed robots.txt file. mtime()¶ Returns the time the robots.txt file was last fetched. This is useful for long-r...

urllib.robotparser/RobotFileParser/read.name: read
urllib.robotparser/RobotFileParser/read.description: Reads the robots.txt URL and feeds it to the parser.

info.excerpt: Reads the robots.txt URL and feeds it to the parser.

urllib.robotparser/RobotFileParser/reque.name: request_rate
urllib.robotparser/RobotFileParser/reque.description: Returns the contents of the Request-rate parameter from robots.txt as a named tuple RequestRate(requests, seconds). If there is no such parameter or it doesn’t apply to the useragent specified or the robots.txt entry for this parameter has invalid syntax, return None. Added in version 3.6.
